feedback.text: I'll write in English, but I am a Swedish citizen. I feel if it's all possible, a addium for video games. Today the video game market is huge, it's a massive industry globally and even in the EU. We got great studios from Sweden like Coffee Stains, DICE, Mojang etc. Take Poland for example which have CD Projekt Red and the Witch Series. EU should help us consumer to however protect our purchases. Today when I buy a game,some games, the publisher or developer can just at any time at any point just remove my access to my game. Even if I bought it, and not a license. Why shouldn't digital goods also be protected such as physical goods? I hope this fairness act can help to strengthen our consumer protection. Look at the Stop Destroying Games initiative, over 1 million EU citizens wish change. A protection from our games gotten taken away from us. I bought Overwatch for example. One day, when Overwatch 2 came out. They just removed my game. I bought it at a physical store. Now my game is just a useless piece of plastic. Why can they just take my bought game away from me? Why can Blizzard (Publisher and developer) just have all the cards and I have nothing to say or protect my purchase? Doesn't seem very fair to me. Thank you for your time reading this! Kind regards!
